serviceDescription.aws: Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP provides fully managed shared storage in the AWS Cloud with the popular data access and management capabilities of ONTAP.
serviceDescription.aws-cn: Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP provides fully managed shared storage in the Amazon Cloud with the popular data access and management capabilities of ONTAP.

components.label: Choose Deployment Type

helpText.aws: Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP provides fully managed shared storage in the AWS Cloud with the popular data access and management capabilities of ONTAP.
helpText.aws-cn: Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP provides fully managed shared storage in the Amazon Cloud with the popular data access and management capabilities of ONTAP.

components.label: Desired storage capacity
components.description: Average amount of data in your file systems per month.

components.label: Percentage of data on SSD storage

description.aws: Enter the percentage of your total desired storage capacity that will be located on the SSD storage tier. The rest of your data will be assumed to be stored on the capacity pool tier.
description.aws-cn: Enter the percentage of your total desired storage capacity that will be located on the SSD storage tier. The rest of your data will be assumed to be stored on the capacity pool tier.

content.aws: You can read more about storage tiers here

components.label: Savings from compression & deduplication
components.description: SSD storage, capacity pool storage, and backups support data compression and deduplication. Enter the % of savings you expect with compression & deduplication.

helpText.aws: Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP provides fully managed shared storage in the AWS Cloud with the popular data access and management capabilities of ONTAP.
helpText.aws-cn: Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP provides fully managed shared storage in the Amazon Cloud with the popular data access and management capabilities of ONTAP.

components.label: Desired additional SSD IOPS
components.placeholder: Enter amount of IOPS per month
components.description: Enter the amount of additional SSD IOPS you would like to provision for your file systems each month. FSx for ONTAP file systems include 3 IOPS per GiB of SSD storage, up to the maximum supported by each file system’s configured throughput capacity. These IOPS are provisioned on top of the default included IOPS.

content.aws: You can read about the maximum provisioned IOPS for each level of throughput capacity here

components.label: Desired aggregate throughput
components.description: Enter the aggregate throughput capacity you want to provision.

content.aws: You can learn more about the available throughput capacities for each file system generation here

helpText.aws: Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP provides fully managed shared storage in the AWS Cloud with the popular data access and management capabilities of ONTAP.
helpText.aws-cn: Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP provides fully managed shared storage in the Amazon Cloud with the popular data access and management capabilities of ONTAP.

components.label: Backup storage
components.placeholder: Enter the amount of backup storage
components.description: Enter the average amount of backup storage consumed for your file systems each month. As backups are incremental, you can generally expect for your backup storage to be roughly equal to the total amount of data, plus the change rate of your data. For example, if you expect to store 100GB of data each month and your data changes by 5GB each month, you should enter 105GB.

components.label: Pricing for ONTAP Single-AZ 2N Backup Storage

helpText.aws: Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP provides fully managed shared storage in the AWS Cloud with the popular data access and management capabilities of ONTAP.
helpText.aws-cn: Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP provides fully managed shared storage in the Amazon Cloud with the popular data access and management capabilities of ONTAP.

components.label: Backup storage
components.placeholder: Enter the amount of backup storage
components.description: Enter the average amount of backup storage consumed for your file systems each month. As backups are incremental, you can generally expect for your backup storage to be roughly equal to the total amount of data, plus the change rate of your data. For example, if you expect to store 100GB of data each month and your data changes by 5GB each month, you should enter 105GB.
